title: pyrus garhwalensis
intro: Pyrus garhwalensis, also known as the Himalayan pear or Bhuramari, is a species of plant that belongs to the family Rosaceae and is native to the Himalayan region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral activities. The health benefits of Pyrus garhwalensis include its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory infections, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and prevent cancer. Herbal preparations of Pyrus garhwalensis include extracts, decoctions, and infusions, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
